question_text: A swimmer experiences a pressure of 40,000 Pa at a certain depth in the ocean. If the pressure increases by 10,000 Pa for every 1 metre of depth, at what depth is the swimmer?
explanation: The additional pressure from water depth = 40,000 Pa - atmospheric pressure (approximately 10,000 Pa at surface) = 30,000 Pa. Dividing by 10,000 Pa per metre gives 30,000 / 10,000 = 3 metres below sea level, so total depth is approximately 4 metres including surface pressure.
